title: Ensure that the --anonymous-auth argument is set to false

input_row.title: Ensure that the --anonymous-auth argument is set to false
input_row.description: Disable anonymous requests to the Kubelet server.
input_row.rationale: When enabled, requests that are not rejected by other configured authentication methods are treated as anonymous requests. These requests are then served by the Kubelet server. You should rely on authentication to authorize access and disallow anonymous requests.
input_row.audit: If using a Kubelet configuration file, check that there is an entry for authentication: anonymous: enabled set to false . Run the following command on each node: ps -ef | grep kubelet Verify that the --anonymous-auth argument is set to false . This executable argument may be omitted, provided there is a corresponding entry set to false in the Kubelet config file.
input_row.remediation: If using a Kubelet config file, edit the file to set authentication: anonymous: enabled to false . If using executable arguments, edit the kubelet service file /etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf on each worker node and set the below parameter in KUBELET_SYSTEM_PODS_ARGS variable. --anonymous-auth=false Based on your system, restart the kubelet service. For example: systemctl daemon-reload systemctl restart kubelet.service
input_row.default_value: By default, anonymous access is enabled.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control can be verified using Kubernetes API or kubectl commands to check the Kubelet configuration for the `--anonymous-auth` argument.
- The audit section specifies using `ps -ef | grep kubelet`, which indicates that the property can be checked through command-line tools, but it does not require subjective review or non-API host access.
- The rationale and remediation sections provide clear guidance on what to check and how to configure it, reinforcing that this is a technical control rather than a policy issue.

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_kubelet_anonymous_auth_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the command `kubectl get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[*].status.nodeInfo.kubeletVersion}'` to ensure that the Kubelet is running and to identify the nodes.
- For each node, check the Kubelet configuration by running `kubectl get configmap kubelet-config -n kube-system -o yaml` (if using a ConfigMap for Kubelet configuration).
- Verify that the `anonymous: enabled` entry is set to `false` in the Kubelet configuration.
- If using command-line flags, run `kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l k8s-app=kubelet -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[*].args}'` to check the arguments passed to the Kubelet.
- Pass/Fail criteria:
  - Pass: The `--anonymous-auth` argument is set to `false` or the Kubelet configuration file has `anonymous: enabled: false`.
  - Fail: The `--anonymous-auth` argument is not set to `false` or the Kubelet configuration file does not have `anonymous: enabled: false`.
